" 
• 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
G. Lewis Chartrand, Jr. 
Nielsen, Merksamer, et. ale 
770 L street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Chartrand: 
April 5, 1988 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-126 
You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions of 
the Political Reform Act of 1974.11 
QUESTION 
Is the name "citizens For No-Fault, Sponsored by the 
California Insurers" an appropriate name for an insurance 
industry-sponsored committee supporting the proposed "No-Fault 
Insurance" initiative? 
CONCLUSION 
The above-mentioned name meets the identification requirements 
for sponsored committees under the Act at this time. 
However, please note that if the measure qualifies for the 
ballot, the committee must include the statement, "a commjttee for 
Proposition __ " in all references to the committee required by 
law. 
The committee must begin including the statement with its 
name within 30 days after the Secretary of State announces the 
ballot number for the measure. 
In addition, the committee's statement of organization (Form 
410) would have to be amended to indicate that the committee is 
supporting the passage of the measure, rather than its 
qualification, and to reflect the committee's new name. 
1IGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seg. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Chartrand, Lewis, Jr. 
Page Two 
FACTS 
The California insurance industry intends to form a committee 
to support the proposed "No-Fault Insurance" initiative. 
A 
majority of the contributions for this committee will come from 
the insurance industry. 
Some funds will be solicited from the 
general public. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 84106 requires a committee to include the name of its 
sponsor in the name of the committee. 
In addition, section 84107 
requires that within 30 days of the designation of the numerical 
order of propositions appearing on the ballot, any committee which 
has as its principal activity the support of a ballot measure, 
shall include the statement, "a committee for Proposition __ ,If in 
any reference to the committee required by law. 
Section 84103 requires that whenever there is a change in any 
of the information conta.i~ed in the statement of organization an 
amendment shall be filed within 10 days to reflect the change. 
If 
the measure qualifies for, the ballot, the nature of the 
committee's activities will change from supporting the 
qualification of the measure to supporting t~e passage of the 
measure. 
In addition, the committee's name will be changed to 
include a statement that the committee supports the measure and to 
include the ballot number assigned to the measure. 
These changes 
• 
will trigger the requirement set out in section 84103 to amend the 
committee's statement of organization. 
If you have any further questions, please call me at (916) 
322-5662. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Co 
sel 
Ke on S. Braaten-Moen 
Eolitical Reform Consultant

In addition, the Committee expects

After discussing the issue with your supervisor, you 
concluded that the above referenced name complied with relevant 
regulations and statutes.

California

G. Lewis Chartrand, Jr. 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
770 L street, suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Chartrand: 
March 28, 1988 
Re: 
88-126 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on March 25, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18 3 2 9) . ) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322~5660
